response:
KEEP THE AREA CLEAN: BRUSH GENTLY AND THOROUGHLY. RINSE MOUTH WITH WATER AFTER MEALS. CLEAN TOOTH TO PREVENT FURTHER DAMAGE.  
*HIGHLIGHTED SECTION*  
USE TEMPORARY FILLING MATERIALS TO COVER THE VOID. FOLLOW INSTRUCTIONS CAREFULLY TO AVOID MORE HARM. BOX MATERIALS CAN PROVIDE SHORT-TERM PROTECTION.  
* * *  
AVOID HARD FOODS TO PREVENT MORE DAMAGE. STAY AWAY FROM CHERRY CAR AND STICKY FOODS. REDUCE RISK OF TOOTH BREAKAGE.  
*HIGHLIGHTED SECTION*  
PAIN MANAGEMENT IS IMPORTANT FOR COMFORT. USE IBUPROFEN OR ACETAMINOPHEN AS NEEDED. AVOID ASPIRIN TO PREVENT BLEEDING.  
* * *  
MONITOR FOR CHANGES IN THE TOOTH CONDITION. LOOK FOR SWELLING OR BAD TASTE. TRADITIONALLY, DENTAL CARE IS ESSENTIAL.  
*HIGHLIGHTED SECTION*  
SEEK DENTAL CARE AS SOON AS POSSIBLE. TWO OPTIONS MAY BE AVAILABLE IN YOUR AREA. DENTAL EMERGENCIES NEED IMMEDIATE ATTENTION.
